steps.title: Recognize the structure
steps.content: A triangular pyramid has 4 vertices and exactly 6 edges. When all 6 edges equal 96 cm, the structure is a regular tetrahedron: all 4 faces are congruent equilateral triangles with side 96 cm.

steps.title: Calculate surface area
steps.content:
Since the surface has 4 congruent equilateral triangle faces with side 96:
$$\text{Surface Area} = 4 \times \frac{96^2\sqrt{3}}{4} = 96^2\sqrt{3}$$
The coefficients simplify immediately. Evaluating: $96^2 = 9216$, so the surface area is $9216\sqrt{3}$ square centimeters. Therefore $k = 9216$.
